2605.23204.title: AutoResearch AI: Towards AI-Powered Research Automation for Scientific Discovery
2605.23204.paper_summary: Scientific research is being reshaped by AI systems that move beyond isolated assistance toward longer-horizon workflows spanning literature grounding, hypothesis generation, experimentation, validation, reporting, and revision. This shift marks a transition from task-level AI for science to workflow-level research automation. Yet current systems remain fragmented, differing in autonomy, domain scope, execution environment, validation mechanism, and human oversight, while still struggling with evidence preservation, reproducibility, weak-direction rejection, provenance tracking, cross-domain robustness, and accountable scientific closure. This survey examines these developments through AutoResearch, defined as the developmental spectrum of AI-powered scientific workflow automation. Within it, Vibe Research denotes the human-steered region of prompt-based assistance and human-verified execution, whereas emerging AI-led systems coordinate larger portions of the discovery loop without achieving robust autonomy. We analyze how research systems redistribute control, evidence, execution, validation, and accountability across workflows and organize the field around five workflow conditions: literature and research grounding; hypothesis formation and planning; experimentation and tool use; feedback, validation, and review; and reporting and knowledge communication. We further synthesize AI scientist systems, mixed-initiative co-research frameworks, benchmarks, domain deployments, and open-source infrastructures. Finally, we propose five evaluation dimensions--novelty, validity, impact, reliability, and provenance--and show that AutoResearch autonomy is domain-conditioned, being more credible in structured, executable, and rapidly verifiable settings but limited in embodied, delayed, heterogeneous, ethical, or institutionally accountable contexts.
2605.23204.paper_summary_zh: 科學研究正受到人工智慧系統的重塑，這些系統超越了孤立的協助，朝向涵蓋文獻基礎、假設生成、實驗、驗證、報告和修訂的長期工作流程。這一轉變標誌著從科學任務級別的人工智慧到工作流程級別的研究自動化的過渡。然而，目前的系統仍然是片段化的，在自主性、領域範圍、執行環境、驗證機制和人類監督方面存在差異，同時在證據保存、可重複性、弱方向拒絕、來源追蹤、跨領域穩健性和可負責的科學結論方面仍然面臨挑戰。本調查通過AutoResearch來檢視這些發展，AutoResearch被定義為人工智慧驅動的科學工作流程自動化的發展範疇。在這其中，Vibe Research表示以人類為主導的基於提示的協助和人類驗證的執行區域，而新興的人工智慧主導系統則協調發現循環的更大部分，但尚未實現穩健的自主性。我們分析研究系統如何在工作流程中重新分配控制、證據、執行、驗證和問責，並圍繞五個工作流程條件組織該領域：文獻和研究基礎；假設形成和規劃；實驗和工具使用；反饋、驗證和審查；以及報告和知識傳播。我們進一步綜合了人工智慧科學家系統、混合主動協作研究框架、基準、領域部署和開源基礎設施。最後，我們提出五個評估維度——新穎性、有效性、影響力、可靠性和來源——並顯示AutoResearch的自主性是領域條件化的，在結構化、可執行和快速可驗證的環境中更具可信度，但在具體化、延遲、異質、倫理或機構負責的情境中則受限。
2605.23204.author: Guiyao Tie et.al.
2605.23204.authors: Guiyao Tie, Jiawen Shi, Dingjie Song, Yixiao Huang, Ziji Sheng, Xueyang Zhou, Daizong Liu, Pan Zhou, Yongchao Chen, Ran Xu, Lifang He, Qingsong Wen, Manling Li, Cong Lu, Shuai Li, Pengtao Xie, Yixuan Yuan, Rui Meng, Lei Xing, Lichao Sun, Caiming Xiong, Philip S. Yu, Jianfeng Gao
